danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile entities positioned in level paths
danger_signals.meaning: Obstacles that deal damage on contact unless jumped on or avoided

danger_signals.visual_form: Horizontal and vertical spike protrusions from blocks and cannon blocks
danger_signals.meaning: Instant hazards that damage the player; MicroMax can fit between horizontal spikes

danger_signals.visual_form: Screen edges and gaps in level geometry
danger_signals.meaning: Fall damage or level reset; navigation challenge requiring precise platforming

danger_signals.visual_form: Specialized ground tiles (inferred from level names: 'Under Skull Mountain,' 'The Black Pit')
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental damage zones requiring specific forms or avoidance

safety_signals.meaning: Safe destination; reaching it completes level and progresses narrative

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible mask objects distributed throughout levels
safety_signals.meaning: Safe transformation items granting new abilities and hit points; Kid Chameleon gains temporary invulnerability during form change
Resource for special abilities; indicates capacity for offense/defense

safety_signals.visual_form: Stable ground and wall tiles (rubber blocks, ice blocks, normal terrain)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe traversal surfaces; different surface types modify movement properties
Safe shortcuts to alternate areas or levels; provide strategic path choices

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mask transformation mechanic represents identity fluidity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence in standard platformer levels; ability to occupy any mask form

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Virtual reality game containment; enemies serve as manifestation
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Various enemy types (Bagel Brothers, heads, flying entities) represent AI's generative hazards
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: 100+ interconnected levels; teleporters and alternate paths suggest algorithmic maze structure
Presumably freed/saved upon reaching level endpoints or narrative checkpoints

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level design spans 100+ discrete zones with non-linear teleporter connections; maze-like interconnected structure suggests digital space rather than physical geography
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No save/password system enforces single continuous run; restart on death resets progression, simulating 'trapped in game' state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player character visual changes to match mask form: Kid Chameleon (base), SkyCutter (surfer board appearance), MicroMax (tiny fly), Maniaxe (axe-wielding), Red Stealth (samurai), Cyclone (flying form), Berzerker (aggressive stance), Juggernaut (armored/heavy), Eyeclops
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Movement speed, jump height, attack patterns, and physics properties shift dramatically per form; SkyCutter cannot stand still, MicroMax wall-climbs, Red Stealth strikes, Cyclone flies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Distinct ground tile textures and colors represent ice/rubber/normal terrain
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ice accelerates movement (~0.4 pixels/frame); rubber reduces max speed; normal surfaces provide baseline physics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level layouts with low ceilings, open spaces, and corridors of varying widths
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: SkyCutter jumps reduce speed in low-ceiling areas; MicroMax zig-zags faster in 1-2 block width tunnels; ceiling height constrains viable form choices

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy animation frames and positioning relative to player
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol, follow player, and attack; Red Stealth can be repeatedly hit via directional strike bug; fast-striking bug enables continuous axe spam (18 frames → 5-8 frames per attack)

environmental_storytelling.example: Levels named 'Under Skull Mountain,' 'The Black Pit,' 'Blizzard Mountain,' 'Caves of Ice,' 'Alien Isle,' 'Frosty Doom,' 'Beneath Twisted Hills,' 'Final Marathon' convey distinct biome/dungeon contexts within VR world
Ice stages suggest environmental hazard escalation; lava/dark pit themes hint at descending into dangerous VR layers
Specific forms required to access certain paths (e.g., MicroMax to fit through spike gaps, SkyCutter to cross wide gaps, Cyclone to reach high areas) shows world design responds to form availability
Non-linear teleporters between levels and alternate paths suggest VR reality is fragmented or player has multiple rescue/escape routes available
'Bagel Brothers' level features specific enemy type; varied boss/head enemies imply AI creates themed challengers per zone

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Crawl (1.0 px/s), Walk (2.0 px/s), Run (3.128 px/s); Jump height varies by speed; air drag of -0.1 px/frame until reaching 2.160 px/s minimum
Reduced gravity; cannot stand still; turns slowly; horizontal speed dependent only on run button; exhibits sine-wave drift due to triple-flip bug
Zig-zags between walls for faster climb; can fit through horizontal cannon spikes; increased air control (hold B to lower jump height even after peak)

Strike animation can be turned mid-swing to hit trailing enemies; jump mid-strike aborts animation but damage applies frames later; fast-strike bug enables attack every 5 frames vs. normal 13-frame cooldown
Retains higher forward speed from ledge bounce/fall while flying; air drag still applies but maintains momentum advantage
Jump cancels throw animation; enables rapid axe spam (every 5 frames vs. 18 normal); alternative rubber block hop method achieves every 8 frames
Walk speed 1.128 px/s, Run speed 3.0 px/s, Jump speed 2.128 px/s; slower than most forms but implied higher hit points
Bouncing off enemy grants speed boost; multiple bounces in succession compound boost; mechanic details marked for investigation in TASVideos data

notes: Kid Chameleon communicates world-state primarily through form-switching mechanics and level design interconnectivity. The virtual reality framing (rogue AI, 100+ levels, teleporter maze) is reinforced by level naming and non-linear layout rather than explicit visual storytelling. Danger signals rely on enemy placement, spike visual design, and pit geometry. Form variants act as faction-less identity markers showing player agency rather than allegiance. Physics properties (ice acceleration, rubber drag, wall-climbing) communicate environmental hazards through gameplay feel. The lack of save system and continuous-run structure is a meta-narrative signal of VR entrapment. No explicit faction warfare or political systems detected; world-state is conveyed through mechanical state (equipped form) and spatial navigation.
